Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "fat_title_id" ; FAT Title::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "funkis_cloud_title_id" ; FAT Title::Funkis Cloud Title id ) & SetProperty ( "title_environment" ; FAT Title::Title Environment )
FAT Title::Title isLive Or FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud"
Not FAT Title::Title isLive Or FAT Title::Title Backend ≠ "Funkis Cloud"
IsEmpty (FAT Title::Base project) Or IsEmpty (FAT Package::Local export type) Or notDeveloper
"View all media with subtitles in title"
"View all text styles defined in title"
Case(IsEmpty(FAT Title::gObjectGroup) ; True; FAT IO::Object Group = FAT Title::gObjectGroup)
"This IO is used " & FAT IO::Number of instances & " times in the Title."
"View all page items using this IO"
"Connected list: " & FAT List @ IO::Name & ". Click to view."
1//FAT IO::IO Group = ExecuteSQL ( sqlQuery ; fieldSeparator ; rowSeparator { ; arguments... } )
"Click to stop monitoring IO in Insight"
"Click to stop monitoring IO in Insight"
"This IO is used as a custom filter in Funkis Insight"
"This IO is used as a custom filter in Funkis Insight"
SetProperty ( "io_id" ; FAT IO::PrimaryKey ) & SetProperty ( "io_type" ; FAT IO::IO type)

StyledText.Data: An IO is a storage for user input during runtime. IO input can be created on Member and Group level and the ouput can be visualised in Member, Group, Session or Event context. Each IO is an unique identifier that can be used to connect different building blocks with each other. It depends on context what kind of connection that is established between the blocks using the same IO id.
Number of IOs in this Title: <<Number of IOs>>
Add IO-variables for managing user-generated data. Building blocks named "Input" saves data and blocks named "Output" displays data. Connect to IOs in Page item Settings menu.
IO for storing user Input and displaying Output (IO).

Calculation.Text: "View all texts in the whole Title"
"Remove IO Group - this will NOT remove any IOs"
"This IO group contain " & FAT Object Group::Number of IOs & " IOs."

StyledText.Data: If you have many IOs - Create Groups and assign each IO a group to make authoring easier. Groups are shared between Lists and IOs.
